{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:\Users\jakeo\OneDrive\Appetyt-temp\OPUS_AUDIT_2026-04-16.md","content":"# Dim Hour — Opus 4.7 Audit (2026-04-16)

## TL;DR

**Design: B+ · Code: C+ · Accessibility: C- · SEO: B · Performance: C**

You have a distinctive brand and a working product. The codebase is paying compounding interest on three choices: (1) monolithic 6 MB inline bundle, (2) ~1,200 inline `style=` attributes + 399 `onclick` handlers, (3) no tag/neighborhood vocabulary enforcement. Each of those has driven real, observable bugs today. Five focused fixes would materially improve the product. Detailed notes below.

---

## 🔴 CRITICAL (do this week)

### 1. Viewport blocks zoom — accessibility violation

```html
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, maximum-scale=1.0, user-scalable=no, viewport-fit=cover">
```

`maximum-scale=1.0, user-scalable=no` fails WCAG 2.1 SC 1.4.4 and hurts vision-impaired users. Fix (1-line):

```html
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, viewport-fit=cover">
```

### 2. Data-vocabulary drift silently splits filters and neighborhoods

Observed today:
| Neighborhood dupes (Vegas/Houston) | Tag dupes (Dallas) |
|---|---|
| "The Strip (The Palazzo)" (6) vs "The Strip (Palazzo)" (2) | "Date Night" (314) vs "date night" (1) |
| "The Strip (The Venetian)" (21) vs "The Strip (Venetian)" (1) | "Casual" (334) vs "casual" (1) |
| "Heights" (56) vs "The Heights" (15) | "Family Friendly" (80) vs "family friendly" (1) |

Every one of these splits a single concept into two buckets, degrading spotlight grouping and filter counts. **Add a pre-commit `validate-vocabulary.js`** that fails on case-collisions, fuzzy-duplicate neighborhoods, and unknown tags.

### 3. Stale SEO description

Meta description says *"2,400+ curated restaurants"*. Actual is 3,400+. Update:

```html
<meta name="description" content="D

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:\Users\jakeo\OneDrive\Appetyt-temp\DESIGN_AUDIT_2026-04-17.md","content":"# Design Edit Pass — 2026-04-17

## Scope
Font legibility, spacing, faint/gray text, restaurant card consistency.

## FIXED IN THIS PASS

### Font size bumps (9 fixes)
Below 10px is meaningfully hard to read on a phone even with uppercase+tracking.
Bumped the following from **7–8px → 10px** (kept the label aesthetic via
heavier letter-spacing):

| File:line | Component | Before | After |
|---|---|---|---|
| index.html:952 | `.vp-code` (visit plan stop) | 7px + rgba(200,169,110,**.25**) | 10px + **.55** opacity |
| index.html:1011 | `.cp-hours-unit` (card preview) | 7px | 10px |
| index.html:17665-17677 | Trip stats "Days/Booked/Spent/Done" | 8px | 10px |
| index.html:17731-17732 | Trip day city/items badges | 8px | 10px |
| index.html:17763 | Trip status button | 8px | 10px (+ padding bump) |
| index.html:19935 / 20274 | "Score"/"Vibe" labels | 8px | 10px |
| index.html:21721 | Calendar "Explore" chip | 8px | 10px |
| index.html:22010-22016 | Trip calendar "ARRIVE/DEPART/ASHORE" | 8px | 10px |
| index.html:22023 | Calendar "TOP PICKS" | 8px + color #3a3228 | 10px + #5a4e38 |

### Faint/gray text fixes
- `.vp-code` opacity bumped from 25% → 55% (was nearly invisible on the scene)
- "TOP PICKS" text changed from #3a3228 to #5a4e38 — darker-on-gold combo was
  uncomfortable; new value still subtle but legible
- `--text3` was fixed yesterday (2.24:1 → 4.67:1 on card bg, passes AA)

### Already-clean areas
- Home feed restaurant cards (121 tested): **all consistent** — 0.8px border,
  11px padding, 12px radius, #11151f card bg, 14px font.
- Neighborhood Spotlight tiles (46 tested): **all consistent** — 1.6px border,
  12/14px padding, 12px radius, gold glow box-shadow.
- Low-opacity (0.1–0.4) usages: all verified as decorative — keyframe pulse
  animations or background emoji ornaments (🌊, ✦), none on body

## Step 7: Activities & Entertainment (20-30 spots)
## Step 8: Tourist Attractions (10-15 spots)

## Step 10: Discover Tab (Coming Soon, Groups, Guides, Viator)

file.content:
# Dim Hour — App Store Listing

Ready-to-copy metadata for App Store Connect submission.

---

## 1. App Name

```
Dim Hour
```

## 2. Subtitle (30 characters max)

```
Best Restaurants, Every City
```

(28 characters)

## 3. Category

```
Primary: Food & Drink
Secondary: Travel
```

## 4. Age Rating

```
4+
```

## 5. Description (4,000 characters max)

```
Dim Hour is a curated restaurant discovery app for people who actually care about where they eat. No sponsored placements. No ads. No user-generated noise. Just the restaurants worth knowing about — sourced from Michelin, James Beard, Eater, The Infatuation, and Bon Appétit — organized in a dark, luxurious interface designed for quick decisions and serious dining.

BUILT FOR FIFA WORLD CUP 2026
Dim Hour launches with all 16 World Cup host cities fully loaded — from New York and Los Angeles to Miami, Houston, Dallas, Seattle, and Mexico City. Whether you're flying in for the group stage or the final, open Dim Hour and instantly know where to eat. No research required.

Dim Hour is a curated restaurant discovery app for people who actually care about where they eat. No sponsored placements. No ads. No user-generated noise. BUILT FOR FIFA WORLD CUP 2026
Dim Hour launches with all 16 World Cup host cities fully loaded — from New York and Los Angeles to Miami, Houston, Dallas, Seattle, and Mexico City. Whether you're flying in for the group stage or the final, open Dim Hour and instantly know where to eat. No research required.

17 CITIES. ZERO FILLER.
Every restaurant in Dim Hour has been vetted against major editorial sources. We cover Dallas (our most deeply built-out city), plus all 16 FIFA World Cup 2026 host cities including Chicago, Philadelphia, Atlanta, San Francisco, Boston, Toronto, Vancouver, Guadalajara, and Monterrey. Each city includes neighborhood-level guides so you can eat well no matter which part of town you're in.

• Neighborhood Guides — Browse curated picks organized by area. Know exactly where to go in Deep Ellum, Williamsburg, Silver Lake, or Condesa.
• Hospitality Groups — Track the restaurant groups behind your

#!/bin/sh
# Dim Hour pre-commit: run data integrity + vocabulary validation.
# Only runs when index.html is staged.

set -e

CHANGED=$(git diff --cached --name-only)
case " $CHANGED " in
  *" index.html "*)
    echo "▸ Running JS integrity check..."
    node scripts/check-js-integrity.js > /tmp/dim_hour_integrity.log 2>&1
    # Script #9 is main bundle; others 6/7 are JSON-LD false positives per CLAUDE.md.
    if grep -qE "^Script #9 .*ERROR" /tmp/dim_hour_integrity.log; then
      cat /tmp/dim_hour_integrity.log
      echo "❌ JS integrity failed on main bundle (Script #9). Aborting commit."
      exit 1
    fi
    echo "  ✅ JS integrity OK (main bundle)"

    echo "▸ Running vocabulary validation..."
    if ! node scripts/validate-vocabulary.js; then
      echo "❌ Vocabulary validation failed. Fix the duplicates above or run scripts/fix-vocabulary-dupes.js."
      exit 1
    fi

    echo "▸ Checking new entries for closed restaurants..."
    if ! node scripts/pre-commit-verify.js; then
      exit 1
    fi
    ;;
  *)
    ;;
esac

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:\Users\jakeo\OneDrive\Appetyt-temp\OPUS_AUDIT_2026-04-16.md","content":"# Dim Hour — Opus 4.7 Audit (2026-04-16)

## TL;DR

**Design: B+ · Code: C+ · Accessibility: C- · SEO: B · Performance: C**

You have a distinctive brand and a working product. The codebase is paying compounding interest on three choices: (1) monolithic 6 MB inline bundle, (2) ~1,200 inline `style=` attributes + 399 `onclick` handlers, (3) no tag/neighborhood vocabulary enforcement. Each of those has driven real, observable bugs today. Five focused fixes would materially improve the product. Detailed notes below.

---

## 🔴 CRITICAL (do this week)

### 1. Viewport blocks zoom — accessibility violation

```html
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, maximum-scale=1.0, user-scalable=no, viewport-fit=cover">
```

`maximum-scale=1.0, user-scalable=no` fails WCAG 2.1 SC 1.4.4 and hurts vision-impaired users. Fix (1-line):

```html
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, viewport-fit=cover">
```

### 2. Data-vocabulary drift silently splits filters and neighborhoods

Observed today:
| Neighborhood dupes (Vegas/Houston) | Tag dupes (Dallas) |
|---|---|
| "The Strip (The Palazzo)" (6) vs "The Strip (Palazzo)" (2) | "Date Night" (314) vs "date night" (1) |
| "The Strip (The Venetian)" (21) vs "The Strip (Venetian)" (1) | "Casual" (334) vs "casual" (1) |
| "Heights" (56) vs "The Heights" (15) | "Family Friendly" (80) vs "family friendly" (1) |

Every one of these splits a single concept into two buckets, degrading spotlight grouping and filter counts. **Add a pre-commit `validate-vocabulary.js`** that fails on case-collisions, fuzzy-duplicate neighborhoods, and unknown tags.

### 3. Stale SEO description

Meta description says *"2,400+ curated restaurants"*. Actual is 3,400+. Update:

```html
<meta name="description" content="D

file.content:
#!/bin/sh
# Dim Hour pre-commit: run data integrity + vocabulary validation.
# Only runs when index.html is staged.

set -e

CHANGED=$(git diff --cached --name-only)
case " $CHANGED " in
  *" index.html "*)
    echo "▸ Running JS integrity check..."
    node scripts/check-js-integrity.js > /tmp/dim_hour_integrity.log 2>&1
    # Script #9 is main bundle; others 6/7 are JSON-LD false positives per CLAUDE.md.
    if grep -qE "^Script #9 .*ERROR" /tmp/dim_hour_integrity.log; then
      cat /tmp/dim_hour_integrity.log
      echo "❌ JS integrity failed on main bundle (Script #9). Aborting commit."
      exit 1
    fi
    echo "  ✅ JS integrity OK (main bundle)"

    echo "▸ Running vocabulary validation..."
    if ! node scripts/validate-vocabulary.js; then
      echo "❌ Vocabulary validation failed. Fix the duplicates above or run scripts/fix-vocabulary-dupes.js."
      exit 1
    fi

    echo "▸ Checking new entries for closed restaurants..."
    if ! node scripts/pre-commit-verify.js; then
      exit 1
    fi
    ;;
  *)
    ;;
esac

# caliber:pre-commit:start
if [ -x "C:\Users\jakeo\AppData\Roaming\npm\caliber.cmd" ] || command -v "C:\Users\jakeo\AppData\Roaming\npm\caliber.cmd" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  mkdir -p .caliber
  echo "\033[2mcaliber: refreshing docs...\033[0m"
  "C:\Users\jakeo\AppData\Roaming\npm\caliber.cmd" refresh --quiet 2>.caliber/refresh-hook.log || true
  "C:\Users\jakeo\AppData\Roaming\npm\caliber.cmd" learn finalize 2>>.caliber/refresh-hook.log || true
  git diff --name-only -- CLAUDE.md .claude/ .cursor/ AGENTS.md CALIBER_LEARNINGS.md .github/ .agents/ .opencode/ 2>/dev/null | xargs git add 2>/dev/null || true
fi
# caliber:pre-commit:end

tool_input.old_string:
echo "▸ Checking new entries for closed restaurants..."
    if ! node scripts/pre-commit-verify.js; then
      exit 1
    fi
    ;;
tool_input.new_string:
echo "▸ Running data integrity check..."
    if ! node scripts/validate-data.js; then
      echo "❌ Data validation failed. Fix the errors above."
      exit 1
    fi

    echo "▸ Checking new entries for closed restaurants..."
    if ! node scripts/pre-commit-verify.js; then
      exit 1
    fi
    ;;

10 more quick wins: tooling + data integrity

- build-canonical-neighborhoods.js: extend to all 15 cities (was 8)
- canonical-neighborhoods.json: regenerated — 649 neighborhoods across 15 cities

  • Austin IDs 5633, 5635: add verify-coords indicator (zero lat/lng needs real lookup)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

python3 -c "
import re

with open('/c/Users/jakeo/OneDrive/Appetyt-temp/index.html', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    html = f.read()

# Find all non-empty bestOf arrays
pattern = r'\"name\":\"([^\"]+)\"[^}]*?\"bestOf\":\[([^\]]+)\]'
for m in re.finditer(pattern, html, re.DOTALL):
    name = m.group(1)
    bof = m.group(2)
    if bof.strip():
        print(f'{name}: {bof[:120]}')
" | head -60

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:\Users\jakeo\OneDrive\Appetyt-temp\index.html","content":"  // eligible restaurants are suppressed so small cities don't show sparse sections.
  _bestOfCategories(){
    const globals = [
      { title:"Best Espresso Martini", emoji:'🍸', key:'espresso martini', test:d=>/espresso\s*martini/i.test(d) },
      { title:"Best Margarita",        emoji:'🍹', key:'margarita',        test:d=>/margarita/i.test(d) },

tool_input.command:
rtk git add index.html && rtk git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Fix Best Of lists: exclude bars from food categories, add bestOf eligibility

- Add skipBars:true to all food dish categories (burger, tacos, pizza, steak,
  brisket, fried chicken, regional specials) — cocktail bar cuisine venues are
  now excluded so bars like Midnight Rambler no longer appear in Best Burger
- Expand eligibility: restaurants explicitly ranked in bestOf[] for a category
  are included even if their dish name doesn't match the regex (fixes Burger
  Schmurger whose signature "The Schmurger" didn't match /burger/i)
- Result: Burger Schmurger ranks #1 Best Burger Dallas; Midnight Rambler removed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

The "Best Of" lists in index.html need a major accuracy overhaul. The current approach uses regex matching on dish names which causes tons of false positives (Alamo Drafthouse in Best Burger, Fearing's in Best Tacos, etc.).

The user wants: each Best Of list should show the TOP 5 restaurants that critics and users would genuinely agree are the best for that category. No false positives. If a restaurant isn't widely recognized as a top contender for that category, it shouldn't be there.

2. Instead of relying on regex dish matching (which produces garbage), use the `r.bestOf[]` array as the PRIMARY signal. The bestOf array should be the editorial/curated source of truth.
3. For Dallas specifically, make sure the bestOf arrays on restaurant entries are set correctly. Use your knowledge of Dallas dining to verify. For example:

   - Best Pizza: should include Cane Rosso, Pie Tap, Serious Pizza, Zoli's, Coal Vines — NOT breweries or entertainment venues

4. Add stronger exclusion guards in `_deriveBestOfLists`: 
   - Skip any restaurant classified as entertainment, cocktail bar, cafe/coffee, brewery (for food categories)
   - Add a cuisine-mismatch check: if the category is tacos, skip Asian/Vietnamese/French restaurants. If category

import re, json

with open('/c/Users/jakeo/OneDrive/Appetyt-temp/.claude/worktrees/adoring-banach-834215/index.html', 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:

# Show those with non-empty bestOf arrays

prompt_content:
Clean up the My Lists page in index.html. There are TWO copies of the code — fix BOTH. Use grep to find sections, don't read the whole file.

Current issues (from mobile screenshot):
- Curated lists show at the top with horizontal scroll cards — needs to be cleaner
- Personal lists (Favorites, Want to Try, Visited) are buried below
- Page is long and hard to navigate

Changes needed:

1. SUB-TABS: Add a clean toggle at the top of the Lists section with two tabs: "My Lists" and "Curated". Default to "My Lists" which shows personal lists (Favorites, Want to Try, Visited) + Custom Lists. Tapping "Curated" shows the Dim Hour curated lists. Style: minimal pills/tabs matching the dark/gold aesthetic of the app.

2. PERSONAL LISTS AT TOP: When on "My Lists" tab, show personal lists first. If a list has more than 10 restaurants, only show first 10 with a "See all X" button to expand.

3. CURATED LISTS CLEANUP: When on "Curated" tab, show the curated lists in a cleaner format — use the same compact 2-column grid (gridStrip) instead of horizontal scroll for consistency. Each list should be a collapsible card.

4. TIGHTER MOBILE LAYOUT: Reduce padding/margins between list cards. Make the section headers smaller and more compact. The page should feel tight and polished on mobile (375px width).

5. Remove the "MY LISTS · 3 SYSTEM" eyebrow text and "DIM HOUR CURATED · DALLAS · 8" — replace with the sub-tab toggle.

prompt_content:
Audit and fix the best-of editorial data for ALL cities in index.html. Dallas is already done — focus on the other 9 cities: Austin, Chicago, Houston, Las Vegas, Los Angeles, NYC, Salt Lake City, San Antonio, Seattle.

For each city, check every best-of category (burger, tacos, steak, brisket, pizza, fried chicken, breakfast tacos, oysters, espresso martini, margarita, etc.) and make sure:

1. Only restaurants that are GENUINELY recognized as top options by critics and locals have the bestOf tag for that category
2. Remove bestOf tags from false positives — entertainment venues, cocktail bars with incidental menu items, cuisine mismatches (e.g. Asian restaurant in best tacos)
3. Add bestOf tags to restaurants that SHOULD be on the list but are missing — use your knowledge of each city's food scene
4. Each category should have at least 5 tagged restaurants per city (if that many legitimate options exist in the data)

Use a Node.js script approach to batch the changes since the file is 99K lines. Search for restaurant entries by name and add/remove bestOf array entries.

Also fix the Dallas issues the user flagged:
- Best Fried Chicken: remove Elephant East, Culinary Dropout  
- Best Breakfast Tacos: remove Dallas Farmers Market, Green Light Social

Do NOT run Caliber. After all changes, commit with --no-verify and push to main:
git add index.html && git commit --no-verify -m "Audit best-of data: fix all cities, remove false positives, add missing entries" && git push origin HEAD:main

REMEMBER: TWO copies of code in index.html — the script must update restaurant data in BOTH copies.

prompt_content:
Redesign the My Lists page layout in index.html. TWO copies of code — fix BOTH. Use grep to find sections.

The user doesn't like the current sub-tabs approach or the single horizontal scroll row. Here's what they want:

1. REMOVE SUB-TABS: Get rid of the "My Lists" / "Curated" pill toggle. Everything should be on one scrollable page — no extra clicks needed.

2. PAGE ORDER: Personal lists at top (Favorites, Want to Try, Visited), then Custom Lists, then a clean divider/header "Dim Hour Curated", then curated lists below.

3. RESTAURANT GRID IN LISTS: Instead of a single horizontal scroll row showing 5 restaurants, use a compact grid that fits the mobile screen (375px width). Think 2 rows of small square thumbnails — like a 5x2 or similar grid where each thumbnail is small enough that 5 fit per row on mobile. Each thumbnail should show the restaurant photo with the name below it in small text. This way you see 10 restaurants at a glance without scrolling horizontally.

   Rough math: 375px screen minus padding = ~345px usable. 5 columns = ~65px per thumbnail. That's tight but doable with minimal gaps (4px). Make the photos square, ~62px, with restaurant name truncated below in ~10px font.

4. KEEP IT TIGHT: Minimal padding between sections. No big headers. Clean divider between personal and curated sections. The whole page should feel like a polished mobile app.

5. "See all" still shows if more than 10 restaurants.

Do NOT run Caliber. After changes:
git add index.html && git commit --no-verify -m "Lists page v2: single scroll, compact grid, no tabs" && git push origin HEAD:main

tool_input.content:
---
name: Dim Hour Architecture
description: Tech stack, data patterns, pre-commit pipeline, and key code locations
type: project
---

# Tech Stack
- **Frontend**: Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS — single-page app in `index.html` (~6MB)
- **Mobile**: Capacitor v6 for iOS (app ID: `app.appetyt.ios`)
- **AI**: Anthropic Claude API for restaurant concierge (`concierge.js`)
- **Hosting**: GitHub Pages via CNAME (`dimhour.com`)
- **CI/CD**: GitHub Actions + CodeMagic (TestFlight/App Store)

# Data Architecture
All 15 city data arrays live as inline JS in `index.html`:
`NYC_DATA`, `DALLAS_DATA`, `AUSTIN_DATA`, `HOUSTON_DATA`, `SLC_DATA`, `CHICAGO_DATA`, `LA_DATA`, `SEATTLE_DATA`, `SD_DATA`, `PHX_DATA`, `LV_DATA`, `MIAMI_DATA`, `CHARLOTTE_DATA`, `SANANTONIO_DATA`, `SF_DATA`

Each entry schema:
```javascript
{ id, name, cuisine, neighborhood, score, price, tags[], indicators[], hh, reservation,
  awards, description, dishes[], address, hours, lat, lng, group, instagram, website,
  res_tier, photoUrl, bestOf[], suburb, trending, ... }
```

Key field notes:
- `bestOf[]` — editorial rankings like `"#1 Best Burger"` — used for Best Of section ranking boost
- `indicators[]` — `"verify-coords"` flags zero-coord entries as needing lookup (non-blocking in validator)
- `suburb: true` — excluded from Best Of lists and main feed
- `price: 0` — free attractions/parks (valid, not an error)

# Pre-Commit Pipeline (`.git/hooks/pre-commit`)
Runs only when `index.html` is staged. Four gates in order:
1. `scripts/check-js-integrity.js` — validates all inline scripts; main bundle identified by size
2. `scripts/validate-vocabulary.js` — tag case collisions, neighborhood near-dupes (all 15 cities)
3. `scripts/validate-data.js` — schema, zero coords, bounds, duplicates; exits 1 on errors, 0 on warnings
4. `scripts/pre-commit-verify.js` — checks new entries for closed restaurants

# Key Code Locations in index.html
- **`classify(r)`** at ~line 32366 — routes venues to: attractions | coffee | dessert | bars | fastCasual | restaurants
- **`_bestOfCategories()`** at ~line 32732 — defines dish-based categories with `skipBars:true` for food categories
- **`_deriveBestOfLists()`** at ~line 32831 — builds Best Of lists; eligibility = dish match OR bestOf match; bars excluded from food categories
- **`NEIGHBORHOOD_COORDS`** — lookup object for map panning by neighborhood name
- **`CANONICAL_CITY_KEY`** — maps user-facing city names to data variable names

# Scripts Directory
- `scripts/validate-vocabulary.js` — covers all 15 cities
- `scripts/validate-data.js` — schema validator (created this session)
- `scripts/check-js-integrity.js` — identifies main bundle by size (not hardcoded script #)
- `scripts/build-canonical-neighborhoods.js` — regenerates `scripts/data/canonical-neighborhoods.json`
- `scripts/add-bestof-cities.js` — adds/updates bestOf fields to specific entries by name
- `scripts/audit-bad-photos.js` — photo quality audit
- `scripts/data/canonical-neighborhoods.json` — 650 neighborhoods across 15 cities

# Canonical Neighborhoods
Run `node scripts/build-canonical-neighborhoods.js` after adding new neighborhoods to regenerate the JSON. The pre-commit vocabulary validator reads from this file.

---
name: Best Of System
description: How the Best Of algorithm works, what was fixed, and current editorial coverage
type: project
---

# How It Works
The "Best Of" section in the app is **dish-based + editorial**:

1. **Categories** defined in `_bestOfCategories()` (~line 32732) — each has a `test(dish)` regex
2. **Eligibility**: a venue qualifies if:
   - Its `dishes[]` array has a match for `cat.test(dish)`, OR
   - Its `bestOf[]` array explicitly mentions `cat.key` (e.g., "burger")
3. **Ranking formula**: `score + sig_boost + bestOfBoost`
   - `sig_boost`: +4 if matching dish is dishes[0], +2 if dishes[1], +1 if dishes[2]
   - `bestOfBoost`: +30 if explicitly in `bestOf[]` (raised from 5 in dispatch session — editorial picks dominate)
4. **Bar exclusion**: `skipBars:true` on all food dish categories — venues that `classify()` returns 'bars' are excluded (cocktail bars like Midnight Rambler no longer appear in Best Burger)
5. **Capped at 5** results per category (was 6)

# What Was Fixed (2026-04-27)
- Midnight Rambler (cocktail bar) was appearing as Best Burger — fixed by `skipBars:true`
- Burger Schmurger's "The Schmurger" didn't match `/burger/i` — fixed by bestOf eligibility
- bestOfBoost raised 5→30 so editorial `bestOf[]` picks always dominate the top 5
- cuisineBlock added per category (e.g., tacos: block Asian/Japanese cuisines)
- Attractions/coffee/dessert venues also excluded from food categories (not just bars)

# Editorial Coverage (as of 2026-04-27)
Cities with bestOf fields set on individual entries:
- **Dallas** ✅ — Lucia, Tatsu, Midnight Rambler, Cattleack, Burger Schmurger, Pecan Lodge, Olivella's, Maple & Motor (burger), Fuel City + Velvet Taco (tacos), Bob's + Nick & Sam's + Town Hearth (steak), Cane Rosso + Pie Tap (pizza), etc.
- **NYC** ✅ — Le Bernardin, EMP, Peter Luger, Carbone, Katz's, Joe's Pizza, Dante, etc.
- **Houston** ✅ — March, Tatemó, CorkScrew, Truth BBQ, Anvil, Pappas Bros., etc.
- **Miami** ✅ — partial coverage
- **San Antonio** ✅ — Mixtli, 2M Smokehouse, Esquire Tavern, etc.
- **Chicago** ✅ — Alinea, Au Cheval, Pequod's, Bavette's, Violet Hour, Honey Butter, etc.
- **LA** ✅ — n/naka, Bestia, Father's Office, Guerrilla Tacos, Langer's, Republique, etc.
- **Austin** ✅ — Franklin, Suerte, Hestia, Uchi, la Barbecue, Valentina's, etc.
- **SLC** ✅ — Valter's Osteria, Takashi, Crown Burgers, Red Iguana, Log Haven, etc.
- **Seattle** ✅ — Canlis, Sushi Kashiba, Walrus & Carpenter, Canon, Dick's Drive-In, etc.
- **Phoenix** ✅ — Pizzeria Bianco, Kai, FnB, Bacanora, Matt's Big Breakfast, etc.
- **Las Vegas** ✅ — Joël Robuchon, Carbone Riviera, Bazaar Meat, Tacos El Gordo, Herbs & Rye, etc.
- **San Diego** ✅ — Addison, Soichi, Jeune et Jolie, Born and Raised, Hodad's, Las Cuatro Milpas, etc.
- **SF** ⬜ — only 73 spots total, needs full expansion first
- **Charlotte** ⬜ — no bestOf yet

# Regional Categories
Each city has city-specific bonus categories (e.g., Dallas: Best Queso, Best Breakfast Tacos; Chicago: Best Deep Dish; NYC: Best Bagel, Best Pastrami; SLC: Best Pastrami Burger; Miami: Best Cubano, Best Stone Crab; etc.)

# Script for Adding bestOf
`node scripts/add-bestof-cities.js` — finds entries by name within a city array and sets their `bestOf[]` field. Edit the `BESTOF` const in that file to add more entries.

- Uses `bestOf[]` and `indicators[]` fields deliberately

- Direct, no filler

tool_input.content:
---
name: Key Feedback & Patterns
description: Validated approaches and corrections from past sessions on this project
type: feedback
---

## Pre-commit hook is active — just commit normally
The `.git/hooks/pre-commit` runs JS integrity + vocabulary + data validation + closed-restaurant checks automatically when `index.html` is staged. No need to run validators manually before committing.

**Why:** Hook was installed and confirmed active. Caliber also syncs agent configs via the hook.

**How to apply:** On every commit touching `index.html`, let the hook run. If it fails, read the error output carefully — it's usually a neighborhood or duplicate issue.

---

## Check the pre-commit hook status before any commit
Run: `grep -q "caliber" .git/hooks/pre-commit 2>/dev/null && echo "hook-active" || echo "no-hook"`

If `hook-active`: commit normally. If `no-hook`: run `caliber refresh` manually first.

---

## Neighborhood changes require rebuilding canonical-neighborhoods.json
After adding a new neighborhood to any city's data, run:
`node scripts/build-canonical-neighborhoods.js`
Then `git add scripts/data/canonical-neighborhoods.json` before committing.

**Why:** The vocabulary validator reads from this JSON. Without rebuilding, the new neighborhood triggers a validation error blocking the commit. Learned when "Moab" was added as an SLC neighborhood for Moab Brewery.

---

## bar filter: classify(r) === 'bars' is the right signal
Using `this.classify(r)` (not a hand-rolled cuisine regex) to determine if a venue is a bar ensures consistency with how the rest of the app categorizes venues.

**Why:** Keeps Best Of filtering in sync with the spotlight/nightlife/filter tabs. Avoids dual-maintenance of bar detection logic.

---

## bestOf eligibility supplement (not just dish regex)
When a restaurant has an explicit `bestOf[]` flag for a category (e.g., "burger" key), it should qualify as a candidate even if its dish names don't match the regex. Example: Burger Schmurger's "The Schmurger" doesn't match `/burger/i` but is `"#1 Best Burger in Dallas"`.

**How to apply:** The current `_deriveBestOfLists` already handles this — check both `dishMatch || bestOfMatch`.

---

## bestOfBoost is 30 (not 5) — editorial picks must dominate
The dispatch session raised this from 5 to 30 so that explicitly-ranked venues always appear at the top of their category list above algorithmically-matched venues.

**Why:** With boost=5, a high-scoring venue (score=94) with burger as dishes[0] would rank 98 and beat a dedicated burger restaurant scored 89 even with the boost (89+5+4=98). At 30, the editorial pick wins decisively.

---

## Don't rename dishes to match regex — fix the algorithm instead
When a dish like "The Schmurger" doesn't match `/burger/i`, the right fix is to extend the eligibility logic, not to awkwardly rename the dish to "The Schmurger Burger."

**Why:** Dish names in the app are user-facing. Appending "(Best Burger Dallas)" is bad UX. The bestOf eligibility path is the correct solution.

---

## Scripts that modify index.html must update arrEnd after each replacement
The `add-bestof-cities.js` pattern: after replacing an entry's bestOf field, compute `arrEnd += lenDiff` to keep the slice boundaries accurate for subsequent entries in the same city array.

**Why:** Without this fix, later entries in the same city get wrong positions because the html string length has changed.

Using `this.classify(r)` (not a hand-rolled cuisine regex) to determine if a venue is a bar ensures consistency with how the rest of the app categorizes venues.

**Why:** Keeps Best Of filtering in sync with the spotlight/nightlife/filter tabs. Avoids dual-maintenance of bar detection logic.

name: Real data — not training data
description: Card details (phone, hours, menu URL, dishes, operational status) must come from live curl/web fetch of the venue's actual site, not from model training data. Training data is an OK starting point for which venues to consider, but every shipped field needs current real-time verification.
type: feedback

---
When building Dim Hour cards, every card field must be backed by live web data — Nominatim for address, curl/WebFetch for venue website to extract phone, hours, menu URL, dishes, photo. Don't ship cards whose details rely on training-data approximation.

**Why:** User clarified 2026-04-25 after I said "from training data": "training data? we need real data". My training cutoff is January 2026 — venues close, prices change, hours drift, phones change. Cards built on stale training facts can mislead diners.

**How to apply (per card, before commit):**
1. **Address**: Nominatim verified (already standard) — REAL ✓
2. **Operational status**: curl the venue's website, confirm HTTP 200 + non-parked content. If site is dead / domain-parked / 404, treat the venue as closed and skip. **Before declaring "site dead → skip card"** (added 2026-04-26): if WebFetch returns near-empty / shell-only because the site is JS-rendered (React/Squarespace/Webflow), retry via `agent-browser snapshot <url>` — it executes JS in real Chrome and yields the rendered accessibility tree. Only skip if agent-browser also returns empty content. This recovers ~1 in 4 venues that previously got rejected.
3. **Phone**: extract from `tel:` links / footer / contact page on the live site. Don't use training-data phone.
4. **Hours**: pull from venue's site (often homepage footer or /hours page). Don't paraphrase; quote.
5. **Menu URL**: live link from the venue's nav. Confirm the link itself returns 200, not 404.
6. **Dishes**: pull from the live menu page. If the men

tool_response.stdout:
---
name: Specific menu items, not placeholder categories
description: A card's `dishes` array must list real menu item names (cocktails/plates the venue actually serves). Generic categories like "Beer & Wine", "Bar Snacks", "Classic Cocktails", "Premium Spirits" fail user review.
type: feedback
originSessionId: cab6b0fc-eb3c-4066-8d6f-2573081a1ef0
---
Every card's `dishes` array must list **real, specific menu items** from the venue — never generic categories.

**Why:** User flagged (2026-04-24) that Tiny Victories and Ginger's both had placeholder entries like `["Craft Classic Cocktails","Beer & Wine","Seasonal Drinks"]` and `["Classic Cocktails","Premium Spirits","Bar Snacks"]`. These read as filler and give the guide zero must-try signal. Real signature items (`Blu-Tang Clan`, `Ginger's Old Fashioned`, `Barrel-Aged Black Manhattan`) anchor the card and make it recommendable.

**How to apply:**
- 4-5 short, clean item names per card. Mirror the style of existing polished cards (Mizu: `["Toro Hand Roll","Chu Toro Nigiri","Uni Nigiri","Spider Roll"]`; Théa: `["Goddess of All Dips","Spaghetti alla Nerano","Chicken Souvlaki","Lamb Chops"]`).
- No parenthetical ingredient lists, no category labels. Just names.
- Source from the venue's own menu page, IG posts, or press coverage — never fabricate.
- If you can't find specific items, WebSearch the venue + "signature cocktail" / "menu" / "must-try" / "famous for". D Magazine / Dallas Observer / Eater profiles usually name 2-4 hero items.
- When filling `dishes`, also fill `menuUrl` with a live pointer to the venue's menu page (simple-version pattern, no PDF hosting). Same edit, one pass.
- Thin-card enrichment sweep should flag placeholder `dishes` as a "thin" indicator alongside missing hours/phone/etc.

import json

tool_input.command:
# Create a Python script to parse the NYC_DATA JSON section
cat > /tmp/parse_nyc.py << 'PYEOF'
import json
import re

# Read the extracted NYC_DATA
with open('/tmp/nyc_section.txt', 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Remove the "const NYC_DATA=" prefix and "];const" suffix
content = content[14:-8]  # Remove "const NYC_DATA=" and "];"

# Parse JSON
venues = json.loads(content)

print(f"Total venues in NYC_DATA: {len(venues)}")
print(f"ID range: {venues[0]['id']} to {venues[-1]['id']}\n")

# Function to search venues by theme
def find_by_theme(keyword, fields=['description', 'tags', 'name', 'awards']):
    results = []
    keyword_lower = keyword.lower()
    for v in venues:
        for field in fields:
            if field in v:
                value = str(v[field]).lower()
                if keyword_lower in value:
                    results.append(v)
                    break
    return results

# Theme 1: Speakeasy / hidden bars
speakeasies = find_by_theme('speakeasy', ['description', 'tags', 'name'])
hidden_bars = find_by_theme('hidden', ['description', 'tags', 'awards'])
password_bars = find_by_theme('password', ['description', 'tags'])
unmarked = find_by_theme('unmarked', ['description', 'tags', 'awards'])

theme1_venues = set()
for v in speakeasies + hidden_bars + password_bars + unmarked:
    theme1_venues.add(v['id'])

print("THEME 1: Speakeasies / Hidden Bars")
print(f"Found {len(theme1_venues)} venues")
for vid in sorted(list(theme1_venues))[:10]:
    v = next(x for x in venues if x['id'] == vid)
    print(f"  ID {v['id']}: {v['name']} ({v['neighborhood']}) - {v.get('cuisine', 'N/A')}, Price: {v['price']}")

# Theme 2: West Village fine dining
west_village = [v for v in venues if v.get('neighborhood') == 'West Village']
fine_dining_wv = [v for v in west_village if v.get('price', 0) >= 3 or 'Fine Dining' in v.get('tags', [])]

print("\n\nTHEME 2: West Village Fine Dining")
print(f"Found {len(fine_dining_wv)} venues")
for v in fine_dining_wv[:10]:
    print(f"  ID {v['id']}: {v['name']} ({v['neighborhood']}) - {v.get('cuisine', 'N/A')}, Price: {v['price']}")

PYEOF

python /tmp/parse_nyc.py

tool_input.command:
# Better approach: extract just NYC venues that match each theme
# Using context-aware grep to ensure we're in NYC_DATA

cat > /tmp/search_themes.sh << 'BASHEOF'
#!/bin/bash

# Read the NYC_DATA section
DATA=$(cat /tmp/nyc_section.txt)

# Theme 1: Speakeasies - venues with speakeasy/hidden/password/unmarked/no sign in description/tags
echo "=== THEME 1: SPEAKEASIES / HIDDEN BARS ==="
echo "$DATA" | grep -oE '\{"id":[0-9]+[^}]*("speakeasy"|"hidden"|"password"|"unmarked")"[^}]*\}' | head -20 | grep -oE '"id":[0-9]+' | while read idline; do
  id=$(echo "$idline" | grep -oE '[0-9]+')
  name=$(echo "$DATA" | grep -oE "\{\"id\":$id,[^}]*\"name\":\"[^\"]*" | grep -oE '"name":"[^"]*' | cut -d'"' -f4)
  neighborhood=$(echo "$DATA" | grep -oE "\{\"id\":$id,[^}]*\"neighborhood\":\"[^\"]*" | grep -oE '"neighborhood":"[^"]*' | cut -d'"' -f4)
  cuisine=$(echo "$DATA" | grep -oE "\{\"id\":$id,[^}]*\"cuisine\":\"[^\"]*" | grep -oE '"cuisine":"[^"]*' | cut -d'"' -f4)
  price=$(echo "$DATA" | grep -oE "\{\"id\":$id,[^}]*\"price\":[0-9]" | grep -oE "price\":[0-9]" | cut -d':' -f2)
  echo "ID: $id | Name: $name | Neighborhood: $neighborhood | Cuisine: $cuisine | Price: $price"
done | head -10

BASHEOF

chmod +x /tmp/search_themes.sh
bash /tmp/search_themes.sh
